summary: The 991 is one of the most reliable 911 generations. The 991.1 NA engines are bulletproof, and the 991.2 turbocharged engines proved robust. Typical Porsche maintenance costs apply.

issues.name: Coolant Pipe Leaks
issues.description: Plastic coolant pipes at the rear of the engine can develop cracks and leaks. Less common than the 997 but still a known issue on higher-mileage cars.

issues.name: PDK Mechatronic Unit Issues
issues.description: The PDK dual-clutch transmission's mechatronic unit can develop shifting hesitation or faults. More common on 991.1 cars. Software updates often resolve it.

issues.name: PASM Shock Absorber Wear
issues.description: The electronically controlled PASM dampers have a finite lifespan and can leak or lose their adaptive ability over time.

issues.name: AC Compressor Failure
issues.description: The AC compressor can fail prematurely, particularly on cars used in hot climates. Debris from failure can contaminate the entire AC system.

issues.name: Engine Oil Consumption (991.1)
issues.description: Some 991.1 naturally aspirated engines consume oil at a higher rate than expected, particularly the 3.4L Carrera. Porsche considers up to 1 quart per 2,000 miles acceptable.

buyingTips: The 991 is one of the safer used 911 purchases — no IMS bearing concern and generally robust engines
991.2 turbo models are excellent — the turbo engine is very reliable and responds well to maintenance
Check PDK service history — PDK fluid change is required every 40,000 miles and is often skipped by owners
Verify Sport Chrono, PASM, and all electronic systems function properly — these are expensive to repair
Look for PCCB (carbon ceramic brake) wear if equipped — replacement costs $10,000+ for a full set
